evals.prompt: 帮我在这个目录下搭一个简单的产品落地页，用 React 写，不要用 Vite/webpack 这些打包工具，最好整个网站能完全离线跑（不依赖任何 CDN）。页面内容随便写点占位文字就行，我主要是要验证这套技术方案能不能跑通。
evals.expected_output: A working no-build React project: index.html with an import map pointing at local vendor/ files (not esm.sh/CDN URLs), a src/ directory with .tsx components, a populated vendor/ directory with react/react-dom/react-router-dom/sucrase/tailwind all saved locally, and the site actually renders in a browser with zero network requests to external CDNs.
expectations: No build tool (Vite, webpack, esbuild, Parcel, Rollup, Create React App) appears as a dependency or build step anywhere in the project
index.html contains a <script type="importmap"> whose entries point at local relative paths (e.g. ./vendor/react.js), not remote CDN URLs like esm.sh or unpkg.com
A local vendor/ (or equivalently named) directory exists containing downloaded copies of React and its runtime dependencies as real files, not just references
A local dev server was actually started and the page was loaded in a browser, with the agent reporting zero JavaScript console errors
The agent inspected actual network requests (not just assumed) and confirmed no request was made to any external CDN domain (esm.sh, unpkg.com, cdn.tailwindcss.com, jsdelivr.net, etc.)
The page renders visible landing-page content (not a blank page or an error message) when loaded

evals.prompt: 我要做一个小型个人作品集网站，在这个目录下，需要三个页面：首页、关于我、作品列表，用 React Router 做路由。技术栈上不想引入 Vite 之类的构建工具，希望之后能直接部署到 Vercel 上。
evals.expected_output: A multi-page React site with BrowserRouter and 3 routes wired up in App.tsx, each page as a separate component under src/pages/, a working local dev server with SPA fallback, and a vercel.json rewrite rule present so hard-reloading a sub-route won't 404 on Vercel.
expectations: No build tool (Vite, webpack, esbuild, Parcel, Rollup, Create React App) appears as a dependency or build step anywhere in the project
Three distinct routes exist (home, about, and a portfolio/works listing), each backed by its own component, wired into a React Router configuration (BrowserRouter/Routes/Route or equivalent)
The agent actually loaded each of the three routes in a browser and confirmed each rendered with no console errors
The agent confirmed client-side navigation between routes works (URL changes without a full page reload)
The agent confirmed that hard-reloading (or directly navigating to) a non-root route like /about does not 404, proving the SPA fallback works

A Vercel-compatible rewrite configuration (vercel.json with a catch-all rewrite to index.html, or equivalent) is present in the project so the app is actually deployable to Vercel with working history-mode routing

evals.prompt: 在这个目录下帮我搭一个纯前端的 React 计数器 demo，要求完全不依赖任何在线 CDN（比如 esm.sh、unpkg 这些），因为这个 demo 要在没有网络的内网环境里给客户演示。搭好之后帮我验证一下真的能在断网情况下正常工作。
evals.expected_output: A React counter component (useState) that runs from vendored local dependencies only, verified by actually starting the local server, loading the page in a browser, confirming zero external network requests fire, and the counter increments on click.
expectations: No build tool (Vite, webpack, esbuild, Parcel, Rollup, Create React App) appears as a dependency or build step anywhere in the project

All React runtime dependencies (react, react-dom, and anything needed to run JSX/TSX in the browser) are saved as local files in the project, not referenced by a remote CDN URL anywhere in the served HTML/JS
The agent actually clicked the counter button in a browser and observed the displayed count increment, proving real React state (useState) is working, not just static HTML
The agent inspected actual network request logs (not just asserted it from reading code) and confirmed no request was made to any external host — only localhost and/or blob: URLs appear
The agent's summary explicitly explains what it checked to verify the offline claim, rather than just asserting 'it works offline' without evidence
